title: Design history dropdown component with version preview
purpose: Provides a dropdown interface for viewing and selecting design history versions, allowing users to preview specific historical states of a design.
module_context: Exports a React functional component DesignHistoryDropdown that accepts design ID and version selection handler props, along with a helper function formatTimestamp for displaying time information.

steps.content: Parse the input timestamp into a date object.
Compare the date with today's date to determine if it's today.
IF the date is today, format and return just the time portion.
ELSE check if the date is yesterday and format accordingly.
ELSE calculate the number of days since the date and format based on whether it's within the last week.
IF the date is more than a week old, format with full date and time information.
Initialize state for dropdown open status and loading timestamp.
Set up a ref for the dropdown element to handle outside click detection.
Use the design history hook to fetch and manage version history.
Determine if history exists and calculate the number of versions.
Set up an event listener for clicks outside the dropdown when it's open.
Handle item click by loading the version content and calling the selection handler.
Render the dropdown button with appropriate styling and version count.
IF the dropdown is open and history exists, render the version list with formatted timestamps.
Set the loading timestamp to indicate which version is being loaded.
Fetch the content for the specified timestamp from the version store.
Clear the loading timestamp after content is retrieved.
IF the content is successfully retrieved, call the version selection handler with timestamp and content.
Close the dropdown after successful version selection.
